text: The surgeon begins by separating the rectum from the vagina, using diathermy to prepare the rectovaginal septum around the visible blue orifice for subsequent suturing. The dissection is performed in a cephalad direction.
The surgeon uses robotic bipolar forceps to gently lift the rectal wall, developing the dissection plane laterally and carefully dissecting the distal portion of the plane.
The surgeon displays the orifice in the vaginal wall, highlighted in red. Single stitches are applied to close the vaginal wall, completing the suture repair.
The surgeon places a biologic mesh over the vaginal suture to cover the vagina. A V-lock stitch is then used to close the rectum with a running suture, completing the repair process.
